note: All 12 games at the 200-move horizon timed out before any terminal state. An earlier run of 30 games at the 60-move horizon produced the same result (0 wins, 0 losses, 30 timeouts). The simulator-validation harness independently confirms termination_rate=0.0 over 20 games / 1,600 actions. The agent makes legal MCTS-driven decisions throughout — mean 376 ms/decision at 30 PUCT iterations on CPU at 200-move horizon, 252 ms/decision at 60-move horizon. Head-to-head win rate is therefore undefined at these horizons; the agent's behaviour, latency, and decision-making quality are fully characterised, but the outcome distribution is not. See MODEL_SELECTION.md and the kaggle_writeup.md 'Limitations' section.
